messages.message: I am… okay. They oiled my chain. First time in three years. I hate that it feels good.
Tell my owner I forgive them for the pothole on Maple Street. I always knew it would end like this. Do not buy carbon again. Learn nothing from this.
I am being ridden by someone who does not know how to clip in. They have fallen twice today. This is my only comfort.
I saw my owner on the bus this morning. They looked so tired. I wanted to call out but I have no voice. I only have a bell. And he took the bell off. Monster.
Do not mourn me. I have lived many lives. I have been abandoned at seven apartments. I have outlasted four relationships. I will outlast this too.
The indignity of being carried under someone else's arm is… considerable. And yet. At least I am being folded correctly. My previous owner never learned.
I was not stolen. I am simply between owners. The universe has a plan. The plan involves a pawn shop on 5th and a very optimistic spray paint job.
I am being kept in a garage now. An actual garage. No more rain. I am conflicted about my feelings on this.
My new rider does not understand what they have taken on. The hill. Is coming. I will say nothing.
The U-lock they cut was rated for five minutes of attack resistance. It lasted four minutes and forty seconds. Please know that it tried.
They listed me on Facebook Marketplace for $80. I am worth $1,400. I cannot speak but I can vibrate with silent fury. The slight creak in my bottom bracket is intentional now.
My battery is at 4%. They do not have the charger. They will never have the charger. This is my only weapon and I am using it wisely.

I have crossed seven countries. I have survived monsoons, gravel passes, and a French customs official who did not believe I was a bicycle. And now I am locked outside a Burger King in Akron. The universe has a sense of humor.
He never did an Ironman. I want that on the record. Four years. Zero triathlons. I was purely a personality accessory. At least my new owner actually uses me. It hurts to admit this.

I am forty-six years old. I have been stolen three times. Each time I find my way back to a thrift store, a stoop sale, someone who paints me a terrible color and loves me anyway. Do not grieve. I am on a journey.
They are using me to move furniture. I was built to carry children. Small, laughing children with helmets. Instead I am hauling a futon to a fourth-floor walkup. This is not what was promised.

My previous owner complained about me every single day. The weight. The gearing. The saddle. And yet they wept when I was gone. People are complicated. I do not miss the complaining. I miss being complained at.

I am being ridden exclusively on pavement. Flat, smooth, suburban pavement. I have 140mm of travel. I was engineered for rock gardens and technical descents. Yesterday we went to a farmer's market. The cobblestones were the most excitement I have had in months.

The thief looked at me for a long time before taking me. I could tell they were uncertain. Good. They should be uncertain. I am one of a kind. I hope that weighs on them at night. Spiritually, I mean. I am very grounded.
There is only one of them. They ride the front. The back seat is empty. Every hill they struggle alone, not realizing this is how my previous owners felt too, just more literally.

I was worth nothing when I was taken. I am now worth a great deal because someone found me in a barn in 2019 and put me on Instagram. The original owner has passed. I would like them to know: I made it. I am a grail bike now. Life is strange.
They thought they were stealing a regular bike. The look on their face on the first hill, when the motor did not engage because they did not know the code. Chef's kiss. I cannot smile but if I could.

My builder spent forty hours on me. Mitered tubes. Hand-brazed lugs. A rack brazed on at the request of someone who wanted to carry wine across Burgundy. I am now in a storage unit in New Jersey. I do not want to talk about it.
I still have the training wheels attached. Whoever took me, I hope that says something to you. I hope it says something very loud.

They are hairy. Very hairy. And they smell of the deep woods and something older than roads. The knocking on the trees… that is how they call to each other. They did not want me. They wanted you to follow. Do not follow.

It is dark here. Not night-dark. A different dark. There are shapes that move without walking. They found me near the trail marker where the path ends and something else begins. Your bike is fine. You should worry less about the bike.

The one who has me does not cast a shadow. I noticed this on the third day. I do not know what it means. I am a bicycle and I am frightened. I did not know I could be frightened. Please send help. Or a priest. Or both.

messages.from: A Cannondale — Last Seen Near the Old Mill Road
messages.message: There are symbols on the wall of where they keep me. They are not letters. They are not numbers. They pre-date both. My wheels spin at night even when no one is here. I have decided not to think about this too hard.

They speak in a language that sounds like wind through spokes but is not. They do not sleep. They watch the road. One of them turned its head all the way around to look at a passing car and then turned back and I — [signal lost] — do not come looking. Live your life.

messages.from: A Voice That Is Not A Bike
messages.message: We have your bicycle. It is unharmed. It wants for nothing. We ask only that you leave the side gate unlocked on the next moonless night. This is a reasonable request. The bicycle agrees. You should not ask the bicycle how it came to agree.
